channel.title: Physio Research Dashboard
channel.description: Weekly breakdown of the latest sports physio research.

item.title: Weekly Research Update: February 15
item.description:
Top Research This Week:

  • Effectiveness of repeated ultrasound-guided suprascapular nerve blocks in hemiplegic shoulder pain: A randomized controlled trial.
    Implication: For physiotherapists managing HSP, while suprascapular nerve block (SSNB) may be considered for short-term pain relief given its promise, it is essential to focus on integrating it within a comprehensive, evidence-based rehabilitation program, as the sustained benefits and safety of repeated applications are not yet established.
    Read Study

  • Postoperative weight-bearing restrictions and rehabilitation after periacetabular osteotomy: a systematic review.
    Implication: Physiotherapists should anticipate and monitor the full findings of systematic reviews on PAO rehabilitation to guide evidence-based decisions regarding appropriate postoperative weight-bearing protocols.
    Read Study

  • Female Representation in Rock Climbing Research.
    Implication: Physiotherapists should be critical of research findings in rock climbing, particularly concerning injury mechanisms, prevention, and rehabilitation, as most evidence is derived from male populations. Tailor assessments and interventions for female climbers based on individual presentation and specific female physiological considerations, rather than solely relying on generalized research findings.
    Read Study

  • Morphological Changes and MRI Characteristics of the Achilles Tendon in Amateur Marathon Runners With Different Running Experience.
    Implication: Clinical implications cannot be drawn from this abstract, as it only describes the study's aim and background, not its results.
    Read Study

  • Female trail running: a systematic scoping review.
    Implication: When assessing female trail runners, physiotherapists should conduct a comprehensive intake to detail menstrual cycle status, hormonal contraceptive use, and nutritional habits, as these factors are often overlooked in current research but are critical for understanding individual physiological responses and injury risk.
    Read Study
